$comment: Per-protocol detail data: governance, audits, incidents. Prototype populates aave, lido, uniswap fully; remaining protocols show the page structure with curation-pending states (full population is M1 work, SAT-298). Provenance tags: 'public-docs' = stated in protocol's public documentation, pending onchain verification; 'onchain-verifiable' = directly checkable onchain.

governance.label: Governance system
governance.value: Aave Governance v3 — onchain voting by AAVE/stkAAVE/aAAVE holders across voting networks

governance.label: Execution
governance.value: Payloads executed via PayloadsController with timelocked executors per network

governance.label: Emergency powers
governance.value: Community Guardian multisig with constrained pause powers, ratified by governance

incidents.description: Protocol paused on several markets after a vulnerability report; no user funds lost; fixed via governance
CRV liquidation left ~$1.6M bad debt on v2 after large borrower position unwound; covered via treasury process

governance.label: Governance system
governance.value: Lido DAO via Aragon, LDO token voting; Dual Governance adds stETH-holder dynamic timelock as a check on DAO decisions

governance.label: Node operators
governance.value: Curated operator set plus permissionless modules (Simple DVT, CSM)

governance.label: Emergency powers
governance.value: GateSeal emergency pause (time-limited, committee-held)

incidents.description: One of nine oracle committee keys compromised (Chorus One); quorum design contained impact, no user funds lost; key rotated
Launchnodes operator validators slashed (~20 validators); losses compensated to stakers per DAO process

governance.label: Governance system
governance.value: UNI token voting via Governor Bravo with timelock; governance controls treasury and fee switch, not pool logic

governance.label: Upgradeability
governance.value: v3 and v4 core contracts are non-upgradeable (immutable once deployed); v4 hooks are pool-creator-chosen, not protocol-controlled

governance.label: Frontend/protocol separation
governance.value: Uniswap Labs interface is distinct from the onchain protocol; UniswapX uses signed intents settled by fillers

incidents.description: Phishing campaign targeted LP NFT holders (~$8M user losses); not a protocol contract flaw

governance.label: Governance system
governance.value: Spark is governed by SPK token holders via Snapshot voting, with all parameter changes routed through the Spark Agent artifact in the Sky (formerly MakerDAO) Atlas

governance.label: Proposal threshold
governance.value: Holders of 1% of SPK supply (100M tokens) or nested contributors like Phoenix Labs can submit proposals, reviewed by the Spark Risk Council before a 3-day Snapshot vote

governance.label: Sky dependency
governance.value: SparkLend stablecoin liquidity is provided directly by Sky and the protocol is a Sky sub-protocol whose parameters tether to the broader Sky Atlas framework

audits.scope: SparkLend deployment verification (Aave v3 fork), original mainnet launch
SparkLend Advanced — oracles and interest rate strategies
Spark Vaults V2 — ERC-4626 vault system

incidents.description: During the KelpDAO rsETH bridge exploit that left Aave with ~$230M bad debt, SparkLend was largely unaffected (~$37K exposure) because governance had proactively halted new rsETH supply in Jan 2026
No direct security exploit or bad-debt event specific to SparkLend or sUSDS identified; Credora maintains a current third-party risk report on the protocol

governance.label: Governance system
governance.value: MORPHO token DAO votes via Snapshot with execution by a governance multisig; controls treasury, fee switch, and whitelisting of LLTVs and interest-rate models

governance.label: Core protocol immutability
governance.value: Morpho Blue's ~650-line core contract is immutable with no admin keys or upgradability, and governance cannot alter deployed markets

governance.label: Permissionless markets
governance.value: Anyone can create an isolated lending market with custom parameters without governance approval, with risk delegated to vault curators depositors choose

audits.scope: Morpho Blue and SpeedJumpIRM interest rate model
Formal verification of Morpho Blue critical functions

incidents.description: Frontend/SDK misconfiguration routed token approvals to the Bundler3 contract instead of its adapters; one bundled tx was intercepted by a whitehat MEV bot and funds returned, frontend rolled back in minutes (no core-contract flaw, no funds lost)

governance.label: Governance system
governance.value: Fluid is governed by the Fluid DAO (rebranded from Instadapp, INST→FLUID 1:1) where FLUID holders vote on rate curves, fees, risk parameters, and protocol allowances after Snapshot signaling

governance.label: Governance flow
governance.value: Proposals progress through community discussion, a Snapshot signal stage, then on-chain execution; the core team transferred protocol ownership to governance

governance.label: Treasury control
governance.value: The DAO actively votes on treasury matters including a proposed Foundation funded by a DAO grant and a treasury draw to cover bad debt

incidents.description: Resolv USR depeg left ~$21M bad debt in Fluid lending markets (Fluid's own contracts were not exploited); covered by Resolv, Fluid treasury, and deferred team revenue
Off-chain Merkle rewards-distribution key compromise drained ~125K FLUID and ~51.9K GHO via empty-proof claims; core lending/DEX contracts were unaffected and keys were replaced

governance.label: Governance system
governance.value: Gearbox is governed by the Gearbox DAO of GEAR token holders, voting via Snapshot on treasury, parameters, and asset/protocol onboarding

governance.label: Delegation
governance.value: Snapshot delegation lets smaller GEAR holders delegate voting power to community delegates

governance.label: Execution & emergency control
governance.value: DAO decisions are executed on-chain through multisig wallets that can pause protocol contracts in emergencies

audits.scope: Gearbox V2.1 and V3 core, governance, integrations, oracles
Gearbox V2 smart contract security assessment
Gearbox Core V3 and Oracles V3

incidents.description: Whitehat reported a UniswapV3 adapter path-parsing bug bypassing health checks; contracts paused via multisig and patched with no funds lost, bounty paid
Forbidden-tokens vulnerability disclosed and fixed; no exploit or loss of funds occurred

governance.label: Governance model
governance.value: EulerDAO is governed by EUL token holders on a one-token-one-vote basis, using Snapshot for off-chain voting and Tally for on-chain execution

governance.label: Proposal process
governance.value: Changes flow from forum RFC to a eIP, then Snapshot/Tally votes covering risk parameters, asset classifications, and core contract upgrades

governance.label: Treasury control
governance.value: The DAO treasury is controlled by EUL token holders through on-chain and off-chain governance and overseen by the Euler Foundation

audits.firm: Multiple (Cantina, Certora, Spearbit, OpenZeppelin, yAudit, Omniscia)
audits.scope: EVK audit reports and $1.25M Cantina code competition, collected in repo

incidents.description: A flash-loan/donation exploit abusing the unchecked donateToReserves function drained ~$197M from Euler v1; the attacker subsequently returned essentially all of the stolen funds

governance.label: Governance module
governance.value: Compound is governed by COMP token holders via Governor Bravo, where addresses holding or delegated 25,000+ COMP can submit proposals

governance.label: Timelock execution
governance.value: Successful proposals are queued in the Compound Timelock contract with a mandatory delay (typically 2 days) before on-chain execution

governance.label: Pause Guardian
governance.value: A designated Pause Guardian can disable Mint, Borrow, Transfer, and Liquidate in emergencies but can never block Redeem or Repay, so users can always exit

audits.scope: COMP distribution system audit (Comptroller distribution logic)
Compound III (Comet) smart contract code assessment

incidents.description: Proposal 062 upgraded the Comptroller with a faulty conditional, over-distributing COMP rewards with up to ~280,000 COMP (~$80M+) at risk, much of it unrecoverable due to the governance timelock
A follow-up drip-function call placed an additional ~202,472 COMP (~$65M) at risk while a time-locked fix (Proposal 064) was pending

governance.label: Liquity v1 governance model
governance.value: Liquity v1 is fully immutable, non-upgradeable, and completely governance-free with no admin key, so nobody can alter the protocol's rules

governance.label: Liquity v2 minimal governance scope
governance.value: Liquity v2's only governance power is directing Protocol Incentivized Liquidity (PIL) from a share of revenue; core contracts remain immutable

governance.label: LQTY staking and voting
governance.value: LQTY stakers vote to direct PIL incentives with voting power proportional to staked amount and growing linearly over time

audits.scope: Liquity v2 BOLD core smart contract audit

incidents.description: No exploit identified against Liquity v1 or v2 itself; the only related incident was a ~$11.6M exploit of Prisma Finance, a separate Liquity fork, via its MigrateTroveZap contract

governance.label: Governance model
governance.value: Curve is governed by a Curve DAO using a modified Aragon Voting App, with on-chain proposals controlling protocol admin functions

governance.label: Voting power (veCRV)
governance.value: CRV holders lock tokens for one week to four years to receive non-transferable veCRV voting power, with longer locks granting more weight

governance.label: Gauge weight votes
governance.value: veCRV holders allocate voting weight to liquidity gauges to direct CRV emissions, changeable at most once per 10 days

audits.scope: Core Curve DAO and DEX smart contracts
crvUSD stablecoin AMM and Controller contracts

incidents.description: A bug in older Vyper compiler versions negated reentrancy protection, allowing attackers to drain several ETH-paired pools (alETH/ETH, pETH/ETH, msETH/ETH, CRV/ETH) for ~$60-70M, much later returned by whitehats and the attacker

governance.label: Token-based DAO voting
governance.value: Balancer governance is conducted by BAL holders voting on proposals (BIPs) via Snapshot, with off-chain votes enacted on-chain through a multisig

governance.label: veBAL voting escrow
governance.value: Holders lock 80/20 BAL/WETH pool tokens for up to one year to receive veBAL, conferring boosted voting weight, gauge rights, and fee share

governance.label: Gauge voting
governance.value: veBAL holders direct BAL liquidity-mining emissions across pools through gauge votes, modeled on Curve's veCRV system

audits.scope: Balancer V2 Vault, pools and Composable Stable Pool
Vault, Composable Stable Pool, and Timelock Authorizer (formal verification)

incidents.description: A critical rounding-error vulnerability in ERC4626 Linear/Boosted Pools was disclosed via Immunefi; most at-risk funds were withdrawn but follow-on attacks later drained ~$2.1M

A rounding flaw in the EXACT_OUT swap path of Balancer V2 Composable Stable Pools was exploited via batchSwaps and flashloans, draining ~$116-128M across multiple chains, with partial whitehat recovery

governance.label: Governance body
governance.value: CoW Protocol is governed by CoW DAO via COW token holders voting on CoW Improvement Proposals (CIPs) through a forum and Snapshot space

governance.label: Token & treasury
governance.value: 1 billion COW tokens were issued at TGE with 44.4% to the CoW DAO Treasury, and inflation capped at 3% per annum

governance.label: Origin
governance.value: CoW DAO and the COW token were spun out of GnosisDAO via GIP-13, with GnosisDAO receiving a 10% token allocation

audits.scope: Gnosis Protocol V2 (GPv2) core settlement contracts

incidents.description: A malicious whitelisted solver exploited a pre-approved interaction in the GPv2Settlement contract, draining ~$166K of accumulated protocol fees; user funds were unaffected and the solver's bond fully repaid the loss

governance.label: Governance token & voting
governance.value: The 1INCH token governs the 1inch Network DAO; only holders who stake 1INCH (gaining Unicorn Power) may vote on or create proposals

governance.label: Proposal process
governance.value: Proposals follow a multi-phase 1inch Improvement Proposal (1IP) lifecycle: forum discussion, temperature check, then a binding Snapshot vote

governance.label: Treasury control
governance.value: The 1inch Network DAO Treasury is allocated by DAO vote, covering funding proposals and deploying new protocols on the network

audits.firm: Multiple (OpenZeppelin, ConsenSys, ABDK, MixBytes, PeckShield, Pessimistic, Coinfabrik, Statemind)
audits.scope: Aggregation Protocol v5 and Limit Order Protocol V3

audits.firm: Multiple (ChainSecurity, Decurity, Hexens, OpenZeppelin, Oxorio, iosiro)

incidents.description: A calldata-corruption vulnerability in the obsolete Fusion v1 resolver contract let an attacker drain ~$5M from resolver contracts only (end-user funds unaffected); the attacker returned the majority after a bug-bounty negotiation

governance.label: ZRX governance token
governance.value: ZRX holders govern the 0x Protocol via the 0x DAO, voting off-chain (Snapshot) on ZeroEx Improvement Proposals (ZEIPs) covering parameters, fees, and treasury

governance.label: ZeroExGovernor (timelocked multisig)
governance.value: Permissioned contract functions are controlled by the ZeroExGovernor, a time-locked multisig requiring 2/3 signatures and a default 14-day timelock

governance.label: Settler / AllowanceHolder model
governance.value: Current swap settlement uses the stateless Settler contracts with Permit2 and AllowanceHolder, replacing the legacy Exchange Proxy

incidents.description: A signature-verification bug in the legacy 0x v2.0 Exchange contract was disclosed by samczsun; emergency-shutdown and patched within ~8 hours with no exploit and no funds lost

governance.label: YFI token holder voting
governance.value: Yearn is governed by YFI token holders who submit and vote on off-chain proposals (YIPs), with majority support required for implementation

governance.label: Multi-DAO structure
governance.value: Since YIP-61 Yearn operates as a multi-DAO of YFI holders, yTeams, and the Multisig under constrained delegation

governance.label: Multisig execution (6-of-9)
governance.value: On-chain decisions are executed or vetoed by the 9-member Yearn Multisig (yChad) acting as Guardian, requiring 6 of 9 signatures

audits.scope: Yearn V2 Vaults legacy core contracts

incidents.description: Attacker used Aave flash loans to manipulate Curve 3pool pricing and drain the v1 DAI vault, causing ~$11M in losses before signers paused the vault within ~11 minutes
A legacy iearn/yUSDT misconfiguration (latent since 2020) let an attacker mint ~1.2 quadrillion yUSDT and steal ~$11.6M in stablecoins; Yearn v2 vaults were unaffected

governance.label: Admin / DAO multisig
governance.value: The protocol Admin grants and revokes admin-delegate roles, governed by Mellow DAO voting and implemented via special-purpose multisig wallets

governance.label: Curated vault model
governance.value: Depositors provide capital while designated curators operate strategy strictly within on-chain constraints and limits set by the vault owner

governance.label: Emergency safeguards
governance.value: Vaults include emergency pause, multisig controls, and timelocked parameter changes configured per vault, with Hypernative real-time monitoring

audits.scope: Mellow LRT core system — treasury, strategy/module contracts, oracles
Mellow LRT smart contracts and MultiVault

incidents.description: No public exploit, hack, or loss-of-funds incident affecting Mellow Protocol identified; the security documentation lists only audits and protective measures with no incident disclosures as of this research

governance.label: Four-role permission model
governance.value: Each MetaMorpho vault assigns Owner, Curator, Allocator and Guardian roles, with the Curator choosing markets and caps while the Guardian can veto malicious actions

governance.label: Curator as chief risk officer
governance.value: The Curator unilaterally defines the vault's investment thesis, selects collateral markets and sets allocation caps, making curator judgment the primary risk vector

governance.label: Named third-party curators
governance.value: Vaults are operated by independent curator firms such as Gauntlet, Steakhouse, Re7 Labs and MEV Capital rather than by Morpho DAO, decentralizing allocation risk

audits.scope: Formal verification of Morpho / MetaMorpho contracts

incidents.description: Following the Stream Finance/xUSD collapse, MEV Capital's Ethereum Morpho USDC vault delisted the Elixir sdeUSD/USDC market and realized bad debt of ~3.6% of vault TVL — a curator-allocation-driven loss
The Stream Finance contagion exposed multiple Morpho curators to bad debt via xUSD/Elixir collateral, though only one of ~320 MetaMorpho vaults had direct exposure (~$700k bad debt)

governance.label: vePENDLE vote-escrow model
governance.value: PENDLE holders lock tokens up to 2 years for non-transferable vePENDLE, which grants gauge voting power to direct emissions plus a share of pool trading fees

governance.label: Transition to sPENDLE
governance.value: Pendle is phasing out the long-lock vePENDLE model in favor of sPENDLE, a liquid governance token with a short unstaking period

governance.label: Gauge-directed emissions
governance.value: vePENDLE holders vote on gauges to channel PENDLE incentives to chosen pools and can collect third-party bribes and LP yield boosts

audits.scope: Pendle V2 — Yield Tokenization and Trading
Pendle V2 Core smart contract audit
Pendle Protocol (V1) smart contracts final audit

incidents.description: Penpie — a separate third-party protocol built on top of Pendle — was exploited for ~$27M via a reentrancy bug; Pendle core contracts were NOT exploited, and Pendle's coordinated pause protected an additional ~$105M

governance.label: Two-DAO structure
governance.value: Rocket Pool is governed by a Protocol DAO (pDAO) of node operators with staked RPL and an Oracle DAO (oDAO) of invite-only members who provide oracle data and caretaker functions

governance.label: pDAO voting power
governance.value: Node operators' effective staked RPL conveys pDAO voting power via a square-root modifier that dampens the influence of large holders

governance.label: Node operators / minipools
governance.value: Anyone can permissionlessly run a node and create minipools (bonding ETH plus RPL) to validate, while rETH is the liquid staking token issued to depositors

audits.scope: Rocket Pool protocol smart contract architecture (pre-mainnet, plus Atlas review)
Rocket Pool protocol contract architecture audit (pre-mainnet)
Rocket Pool protocol contract architecture audit (pre-mainnet)

incidents.description: Two oDAO nodes operated by the Rocket Pool team were compromised via a hijacked workstation with unencrypted SSH keys, draining ~14.75 ETH plus RPL from those node accounts; the protocol itself was unaffected
An ETH-transfer attack against the Atlas upgrade's distributeBalance logic was responsibly disclosed via Immunefi and patched before deployment, with no user funds lost
